# [CHECKLIST TYPE] Checklist: [FEATURE NAME]

**Purpose**: [Brief description of what this checklist covers]
**Created**: [DATE]
**Feature**: [Link to spec.md, plan.md, board item, or relevant documentation]

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based
on feature context and requirements.

<!--
  The /speckit.checklist command MUST replace the sample items below with
  context-specific checks. Do not keep sample items in generated checklists.
-->

## Constitution Alignment

- [ ] CHK001 Angular and TypeScript rules are reflected in the design
- [ ] CHK002 Accessibility expectations are explicit for every affected UI flow
- [ ] CHK003 Feature boundaries keep domain, application, infrastructure, and
      presentation responsibilities clear
- [ ] CHK004 Nostr auth/security rules are preserved where relevant
- [ ] CHK005 Verification uses only `bun run ...` repo scripts

## User Experience

- [ ] CHK006 Loading, disabled, success, failure, timeout, cancellation, and
      retry states are defined where applicable
- [ ] CHK007 Keyboard behavior, focus handling, accessible names, and contrast
      are covered
- [ ] CHK008 Mobile PWA and desktop web behavior are distinguished when relevant

## Auth, Storage, and Backend

- [ ] CHK009 NIP-07, NIP-46, bunker, and NIP-98 responsibilities are not mixed
- [ ] CHK010 Secrets, tokens, auth URLs, and sensitive restore data are redacted
      or never logged
- [ ] CHK011 Storage persistence, migration, and environment variables are
      documented when data behavior changes
- [ ] CHK012 Protected endpoints keep their external behavior and NIP-98
      protection

## Verification

- [ ] CHK013 Targeted tests exist or a clear non-applicability reason is stated
- [ ] CHK014 `bun run format:check`, `bun run lint`, `bun run lint:css`,
      `bun run typecheck`, `bun run test`, `bun run build`, or `bun run check` are
      selected according to risk
- [ ] CHK015 Manual device/browser validation is listed when automated coverage
      cannot prove the behavior

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add findings inline with file paths when possible
- Link back to the active board, spec, plan, ADR, or reference document
